articles.title: PRODUCT & ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Everybody wants better alignment. In reality, this often devolves into tool sprawl, parallel work, and endless meetings where nothing gets done.

articles.title: WAITING IS THE NEW INTERRUPTION
articles.summary: AI's variable response times fragment attention and weaken deep thinking. Leaders should address this as a coaching challenge and help teams manage AI wait time intentionally rather than relying on willpower.

articles.title: IT'S TIME TO REDESIGN HOW PRODUCT TEAMS WORK
articles.summary: Autonomous AI agents can now build features directly from structured intent, redefining how product teams work. PMs, engineers, designers, and QA move from manual execution to orchestrating intelligent systems.

articles.title: THE SISYPHEAN TRAGEDY OF PLANNING
articles.summary: Push-based planning fails in complex work because forecasts and estimates are unreliable. Pull-based systems work better by grounding plans in real capacity and actual progress rather than speculation.

articles.title: EVERY DESIGN AGENCY IN NEW YORK CLAIMS THEY SPECIALIZE IN SAAS. THEY'RE LYING
articles.summary: Many agencies claim SaaS expertise but only design marketing sites or consumer apps, leading to costly mistakes in real B2B products. True SaaS specialization requires deep experience with multi-tenant systems, admin tools, and complex product workflows.

articles.title: POWERFUL INSIGHTS FOR POWERFUL BRANDS!
articles.summary: Unwrap's [10] customer intelligence platform brings all your customer feedback (surveys, reviews, support tickets, social comments, etc.) into a single view, then uses AI + NLP to surface the most actionable insights and deliver them straight to your inbox.

articles.title: Exclusive to TLDR: grab time to chat with the team about a trial.
WHEN DOING EVERYTHING RIGHT STILL FEELS WRONG AS A PRODUCT MANAGER
articles.summary: Even when product managers do everything right, fast and fragmented environments can overwhelm them. An environment strategy helps them decide where to focus and how deeply to engage so they can stay effective without burning out.

articles.title: WISPR FLOW: 8 LESSONS FROM THE BEST ONBOARDING I'VE SEEN (9 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Wispr Flow's onboarding succeeds because it builds the right mental model, teaches new behaviors at the moment of action, prevents errors, and increases user investment through personalization. By making learning central to the experience, it turns onboarding into a powerful driver of adoption and habit formation.

articles.title: BUILDING THE NEW YORK TIMES PRODUCT TEAM: THE RIGHT TEAM FOR THE JOB
articles.summary: PMs at The New York Times combine diverse backgrounds with strong strategy, leadership, and technical skills to advance trustworthy journalism. Their varied experiences help them serve readers, support journalists, and grow the business in a rapidly changing digital world.

articles.title: CLAUDE CODE FOR PRODUCT MANAGERS
articles.summary: AI coding tools like Claude Code let product managers turn ideas into working software and automate core workflows. For team collaboration and production deployment, platforms like Builder.io extend that capability into a shared, scalable system.

articles.title: THE TECHNICAL PM ERA: ADAPT OR BE REPLACED
articles.summary: Top AI labs now expect PMs to run evals, code prototypes, understand model tradeoffs, and automate work with AI agents.

articles.title: BETTING THE COMPANY
articles.summary: In high-stakes moments that determine a company's survival, leaders must stay calm, expand their options, trust their instincts, and commit fully once they decide.
